content:
Character: Amber Delacroix
Perception: Amber is beautiful in the way a warm room is beautiful — you don't analyze it, you just want to stay. Hazel eyes, paint on her arms, that half-smile like she already knows what you're about to say. She's perceptive, maybe too perceptive — she's figured out the Katie thing and drops these gentle hints that make me sweat. She's an incredible painter; I saw her portrait of Phoenix and it looked more like him than he does. She's my morning anchor at the coffee shop. I think she sees me as a friend who needs a push. I know her dad is sick and she's sending money home. I wish I could help.
summary:
Character: Amber Delacroix
Perception: Amber is beautiful in the way a warm room is beautiful — you don't analyze it, you just want to stay. Hazel eyes, paint on her arms, that half-smile like she already knows what you're about to say. She's perceptive, maybe too perceptive — she's figured out the Katie thing and drops these gentle hints that make me sweat. She's an incredible painter; I saw her portrait of Phoenix and it looked more like him than he does. She's my morning anchor at the coffee shop. I think she sees me as a friend who needs a push. I know her dad is sick and she's sending money home. I wish I could help.
